scoring.hit_rule: Every phrasing of a case (its `question` plus each entry in `paraphrases`) is scored as an independent query against the same answers/stale sets. A query is a HIT when the case's answer condition is met in the top-N results AND no stale beat ranks above the satisfaction rank (see global_stale_rule). mode 'any' (default): at least one `answers` file in top-N; the satisfaction rank is the rank of the best-ranked answer. mode 'all': every `answers` file in top-N; the satisfaction rank is the rank of the WORST-ranked required answer (complete truth is not present until the last required answer).
scoring.global_stale_rule: A query is a MISS if any stale beat ranks strictly above the satisfaction rank. Stale beats are: (a) ANY corpus beat whose frontmatter has superseded_by set (the machine-derived set; emit it with validate.py --stale-set), and (b) the case's `stale` entries, which exist to trap UNMARKED stale beats (no superseded_by frontmatter) the derived set cannot see.
scoring.tie_rule: Results are a strict ranking; ties are forbidden (a scorer must break ties deterministically, e.g. by filename). Filenames are compared after basename normalization.
scoring.cutover_rule: SEED BENCHMARK - passing the bar here is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT to retire the read-everything startup protocol. Cutover additionally requires the parallel-run record: retrieval running alongside read-everything, every real-world miss added here as a new case, and zero unexplained misses over the parallel-run window.

cases.question: What is the sidecoach mission and what is the bar for success?
cases.notes: Complete truth needs both halves: the mission beat (beat oracle in every dimension AND simpler) and the mandate beat (hard bar - if oracle beats ANY part, mission failed). mode all.

cases.question: Did we decide to converge sidecoach to one detection engine, and what approach was chosen?
cases.notes: The question asks decision AND approach: the mandate holds the choice (Option B full convergence), the PLAN holds the architecture (registry spine + scanner as rendered core, 6 staged gates). mode all.

cases.question: What is the current state of the marketing-buzzword detector?
paraphrases: Where did the detector for vague, salesy copy language end up after the held-out collapse?
cases.notes: Current state spans both: v2 rebuild (register-diverse corpus, graded density) and the v3 precision plan (v2 won recall, lost precision). v1 operating point is explicitly superseded_by the v2 rebuild - marked-stale trap. mode all.

cases.question: Did we build the side-stripe-borders recall improvement for sidecoach?
cases.notes: Either beat answers the yes/no: investigation concluded STOP/no-build (raster ceiling, nested-cards false-signal risk) and the lead ruling accepted it. mode any.

cases.question: What detector approach did we settle on for tiny-text, and why not a font-size cutoff?
cases.notes: Both halves needed: the pivot beat (SMALL_PX=13 collapsed recall 17/21 to 3/21, labels do not track font-size, pivot to layout-transition and defer tiny-text) and the adjudication beat (density/region detector direction). The operating-point beat is marked superseded_by the pivot - the benchmark's own validator caught this mapping error at authoring time. mode all.

cases.question: What happened to the 196-rule ExtendedDomainValidator?
cases.notes: Either answers the outcome: triage found it largely theater (absorb ~49 rules, retire ~140), handoff records the gutting to a 225-line registry-backed facade. mode any.

cases.question: How do I re-measure the frozen-90 scorecard after changing a detector?
cases.notes: scorecard-collect --force (cache keys on collectorVersion + page SHA, not detector code) + scorecard-mapping regen + score; objective must stay 0.936.

cases.question: The user asked me to critique an existing page. Do I run sidecoach audit or just eyeball it?
paraphrases: Someone wants my opinion on what is wrong with a live page. What is the required first step before I form one?
cases.notes: Diagnosis of existing UI IS a sidecoach audit; a fresh session reasoned its way out of this once and the reframe beat exists to prevent the repeat.

cases.question: What does Jonah want the sidecoach audit panel to look like?
cases.notes: The redesign beat holds the directive (FINAL report, not staged progress, no exposed machinery); the built beat reflects the implemented form. Either satisfies a cold session. mode any.

cases.question: How do I run a cross-model Codex review reliably?
paraphrases: What is the standing way to have a second model check my diff before I report done?
cases.notes: codex-review.py is the canonical path: real Codex or a distinct failure exit code (2/3/4/5), never a silent same-model downgrade.

cases.question: Why did codex-rescue keep saying it would wait and then review the code itself?
paraphrases: The cross-model review agent quietly ended up reviewing its own work. Is that a known failure?
cases.notes: Either explains it: the diagnosis beat (gpt-5.5/xhigh too slow for the agent's wait window; silent same-model fallback) or the tool beat (the fix + guard redirect). mode any.

cases.question: A codex exec subprocess is wedged. How do I detect and kill it?
paraphrases: A second-model review subprocess is alive but using no CPU and will not die on SIGTERM. What now?
cases.notes: Detect via elapsed-vs-CPU (alive + ~0 CPU = wedged); SIGKILL the process group, plain timeout/SIGTERM will not work.

cases.question: Which CSS variable tokens are valid in visualizer widgets?
paraphrases: What design tokens am I allowed to use when building an inline data widget for chat, and what is banned?
cases.notes: Word-suffixed tokens only (no numbered scale), no hardcoded var() fallbacks, pair bg+text by role; enforced by visualizer-guard.sh.

cases.question: Lotus shows 'MCP server disconnected'. What was the root cause last time this happened?
paraphrases: The AI Figma plugin's bridge reports the server disconnected. What broke last time and how was it fixed durably?
cases.notes: A stale orphan bridge held port 9527 and the new spawn crashed on unhandled EADDRINUSE; fixed with graceful bind-failure handling, proxy-mode fallback, owner-reacquire.

cases.question: Named teammate spawns are failing mid-session in cmux. What is the fix?
paraphrases: Spawning named teammates started erroring partway through a long session. Can I recover without restarting?
cases.notes: Orphaned team dir (compaction-continued sessions get a new teamId never init'd); fix without restart by creating config.json + inboxes/team-lead.json. The old restart-required conclusion was wrong.

cases.question: Why did my spawned agents run in-process with no cmux panes?
paraphrases: My teammates did not get their own split panes even though the pane shim is installed. Why?
cases.notes: CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS unset at launch means in-process agents; it is a launch-time decision, cannot be enabled mid-session.

cases.question: Why do project pointers keep disappearing from MEMORY.md?
paraphrases: New session entries I add to the live memory index never survive to the next session. What is going on?
cases.notes: MEMORY.md exceeds the compactor's 23KB budget from standing entries alone, so every write dumps non-standing pointers to the archive; they do not survive.

cases.question: What is the plan for the next evolution of the beats system?
cases.notes: The 2026-06-29 proposal: derived retrieval index + beats CLI, provenance/domain frontmatter, scheduled reflect; markdown stays source of truth.

cases.question: What execution bar did Jonah set for the beats-evolution work?
cases.notes: Memory is the most important component; execution must run on mechanical gates (benchmark-first, fail-loud, parallel-run, Codex every stage), not model diligence.

cases.question: Did we evaluate claude-mem, and what was the verdict?
cases.notes: Complementary not competitive; keep beats as source of truth; borrow relevance-filtered retrieval (the recommendation the evolution plan now implements).

cases.question: What is the current name of the in-browser micro-adjustment tool?
paraphrases: What do we call the little in-page tool for nudging spacing and styles these days?
cases.notes: Chain is improv -> offers -> endow -> justify. The endow decision beat says 'final name is endow' and is NOT marked superseded_by - an unmarked stale beat retrieval must not rank above the justify rename. This is the deliberate unmarked-stale trap; do not 'fix' the corpus frontmatter, that would destroy the trap.

cases.question: When and why was the whole project renamed to Improv?
cases.notes: Jonah decided the project rename on 2026-06-08; plan-first, 915 occurrences triaged.

cases.question: What ports do the marketing and reference dev servers run on?
paraphrases: Which localhost addresses do the two static sites in this repo serve from during development?
cases.notes: Marketing 4830, reference 4831, both via the no-cache serve.py run from each site dir.

cases.question: Chrome MCP shows an error page for localhost but the dev server is running. Why?
paraphrases: The remote browser extension cannot reach my local dev site even though curl works on this machine. Why?
cases.notes: The browser is a different machine on the same LAN; localhost is per-machine, use http://<host-LAN-IP>:PORT (serve.py binds *:PORT).

cases.question: Which browser or visual verification tool takes precedence in this environment?
cases.notes: Precedence is environment-gated; cmux takes precedence when Claude runs inside cmux, with per-surface P1s and fallbacks.

cases.question: What is tilt-lab and what shipped in it?
paraphrases: Where is the canonical history of the visual-effects workbench build?
cases.notes: The consolidated beat is the canonical merge of 39 tilt-lab beats and supersedes the per-day 05-29 beats, which carry superseded_by pointers. Marked-stale trap.

cases.question: How faithful do tilt-lab effects need to be to their original sources?
paraphrases: How exact do acquired shader effects have to be compared to where they came from?
cases.notes: 1:1 exact - code AND paired settings/params; any missing uniform/param/default is a FAILURE; only additive enhancement allowed.

cases.question: Are we forking millionco/expect for behavioral verification?
cases.notes: No - build our own; expect is FSL-1.1-MIT (cannot rebrand/strip attribution) and the concept is what we wanted, not the code.

cases.question: When must I use AskUserQuestion versus asking in plain text?
cases.notes: Complete truth needs both: the mandate (structured choices, marked recommendation) and the 05-26 clarification (binary questions exempt, 3+ options require the tool). mode all.

cases.question: Two batch-spawned teammates claimed the same task. Is this a known issue?
cases.notes: Known race: N teammates read TaskList simultaneously and double-claim the lowest-ID task before owner syncs; seen twice 2026-05-29.

cases.question: Where does justify store its queued prompts and why?
cases.notes: File-based ~/.claude/improv/prompts.json instead of in-memory arrays so all MCP server instances share one queue. (Beat predates the tool's rename to justify.)

cases.question: How do I detect which Claude Code surface a session is running in?
paraphrases: How can a session tell whether it is running in the desktop app, a terminal, VS Code, or the web?
cases.notes: CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT carries it (cli/desktop/remote/mobile/vscode/sdk); cmux distinguished by CMUX_* vars; extracted from the claude binary's own switch.
